review_text: Absolutely love this project management tool! Our team of 12 has been using it for about six months now, and the difference in our productivity is night and day. The Kanban boards are intuitive, the timeline view actually makes sense, and the ability to create custom workflows has saved us countless hours. The templates library is particularly impressive - we found a sprint planning template that we barely had to modify. Integration with Slack means we never miss an update. Highly recommend for any mid-size team looking to level up their project management game.

Good tool overall. The interface is clean and modern, which makes onboarding new team members pretty painless. I especially like the calendar sync feature and the way dependencies are visualized. My only gripe is that the mobile app feels like an afterthought - it's sluggish and missing several features that are available on desktop. If they could bring the mobile experience up to par, this would easily be a 5-star product. Also, the pricing for larger teams gets steep quickly.

It's decent but not without issues. The search functionality is frustratingly slow when you have thousands of tasks. I've been waiting 10+ seconds for results on more than one occasion. The UI is generally nice, but some of the nested menus are confusing - it took me weeks to figure out where certain settings were hidden. On the plus side, customer support has been responsive and helpful whenever I've reached out. The API documentation could use some work though.

Terrible experience. Lost an entire project's worth of data after what they called a "sync issue." Customer support took 3 days to respond and basically said there was nothing they could do. For a tool that charges premium prices, this is unacceptable. We've since moved to a competitor and haven't looked back. Save yourself the headache and avoid this product.

This tool has transformed how our remote team collaborates. The real-time editing features are seamless, and I love that you can see who's viewing a document at any time. The notification system is well-designed - not too noisy, not too quiet. We've integrated it with our CI/CD pipeline through the API, and it works beautifully. The custom fields feature is a game-changer for tracking our specific KPIs.

Solid project management solution. Been using it for quarterly planning and sprint management. The Gantt chart view is one of the best I've seen - smooth, responsive, and actually useful. Drag and drop works flawlessly. The reporting dashboard gives our stakeholders exactly what they need. Docking one star because the learning curve for advanced features is steeper than expected, and some of the documentation assumes too much prior knowledge.

I wanted to like this product, but the performance issues are a dealbreaker. Every time our board exceeds about 200 tasks, everything slows to a crawl. The browser tab starts consuming over 2GB of RAM. I've reported this multiple times and keep getting told it's "being worked on." Meanwhile, we're paying enterprise prices for a tool that can't handle enterprise workloads. The concept is great but the execution needs serious improvement.

Perfect for our agency. We manage 30+ client projects simultaneously, and this tool handles it all without breaking a sweat. The workspace organization is excellent - each client gets their own space with customized views. The guest access feature means clients can check progress without us having to send constant updates. The template system has standardized our onboarding process beautifully.

Mixed feelings. The collaboration features are top-notch, and I love the ability to comment on specific tasks with threaded discussions. However, the pricing model feels predatory. Basic features that should be standard are locked behind the "Business" tier. Want custom fields? Pay more. Want timeline view? Pay more. Want automations? You guessed it - pay more. The free tier is essentially a demo.

Great tool for managing cross-functional projects. The ability to create different views of the same data (list, board, calendar, timeline) is incredibly useful for different stakeholders. Our engineers prefer the list view while PMs love the timeline. One area for improvement: the notification settings need more granularity. I get bombarded with notifications for minor updates that don't need my attention.

The onboarding experience was awful. Despite watching all the tutorial videos and reading the docs, it took our team nearly a month to feel comfortable with the basic features. The UI might look modern, but it's not intuitive. Too many clicks to do simple things. When I reached out to support for help, they just pointed me back to the same documentation I'd already read. Not worth the premium price tag.

Switched from Trello and never looked back. The depth of features here is incredible while still maintaining a clean interface. The automation builder is particularly powerful - we've automated our entire sprint ceremony workflow. Tasks automatically move between boards, assignees get notified, and status reports generate themselves. The time tracking integration is also surprisingly good.

Very capable tool. The search across all projects is powerful once you learn the query syntax. I appreciate the keyboard shortcuts - they make navigation lightning fast. The API is well-documented and we've built some nice internal integrations. Two things I'd improve: 1) The mobile app needs offline support, and 2) The CSV export feature doesn't include all custom fields.

Not suitable for large organizations. We tried to roll this out across our 500-person company and hit scaling issues immediately. The admin panel is too basic for enterprise needs - no SSO support on the plan we could afford, limited user management capabilities, and the audit log is essentially useless. Their enterprise sales team was pushy but couldn't address our technical concerns.

I'm a freelance consultant and this tool is perfect for managing my clients' projects. The portfolio view gives me a bird's-eye view of everything happening across all my engagements. I love the custom status options and the ability to create automations that trigger based on status changes. The calendar integration works seamlessly with Google Calendar. Customer support has been excellent - fast and knowledgeable.

Decent tool but nothing revolutionary. It does what it says on the tin - helps you manage projects and tasks. The UI is clean and the core features work well. However, I don't see what justifies the price premium over free alternatives. The integrations are nice but limited to popular tools. If you're using niche industry software, don't expect native integration support. You'll need to build your own through the API.

Our marketing team has been using this for campaign management and it's been great. The template gallery has some excellent marketing-specific templates. The Gantt chart helps us visualize our content calendar perfectly. I love the custom fields for tracking metrics like engagement rate and conversion. The only downside is that the reporting could be more robust - we still need to export to Excel for complex analysis.

Crashed during our most important product launch. We had a critical sprint board that simply stopped loading right when we needed it most. The status page showed everything was "operational" while our team was completely blocked. We lost 4 hours of productivity during a crucial window. Their post-mortem email was generic and offered no real explanation. Trust is broken.

The API is fantastic. As a developer, I appreciate how well-documented and consistent it is. We've built a custom integration that syncs our GitHub issues directly with project tasks, and it's been running flawlessly for months. Rate limits are reasonable, webhook support is solid, and the API versioning gives me confidence that our integrations won't break unexpectedly.

Very good for team collaboration. The real-time co-editing of documents within the tool means we don't need a separate doc tool for meeting notes and specs. The mention system works well for getting people's attention. I'd give 5 stars but the search within documents is basic - no regex support and it doesn't search inside attached files. Also, the 25MB file attachment limit is restrictive.

It's fine. Gets the job done but nothing special. I've used Asana, Monday.com, and ClickUp, and this falls somewhere in the middle. The interface is probably the nicest of the bunch, but it lacks some power features that advanced users expect. The formula fields are limited compared to competitors, and there's no built-in time tracking without a third-party integration.

Outstanding customer support experience! I had an issue with data migration from our old tool, and their support team went above and beyond. They scheduled a video call, walked me through the process, and even wrote a custom import script for our unusual data format. The tool itself is excellent, but the human touch in their support really sets them apart.

The mobile app is barely functional. I travel frequently and need to manage projects on the go. The app crashes at least once per session, doesn't support offline editing, and the interface is so cramped that I can barely read task descriptions. It feels like a web wrapper rather than a native app. For a product at this price point, the mobile experience is embarrassingly poor.

Great for software development teams. The sprint planning features are well-thought-out. I particularly like the burndown charts and velocity tracking. The integration with GitHub is seamless - commits automatically link to tasks and PRs update task statuses. The backlog management view is excellent. Would love to see built-in code review features in the future.

The collaboration features are good, but the notification system needs a complete overhaul. I receive hundreds of notifications daily, and despite tweaking every setting I can find, I can't get it under control. The "mute" feature doesn't really mute - it just reduces frequency. I've resorted to turning off all notifications and manually checking for updates, which defeats the purpose of a collaboration tool.

We switched our entire company of 50 people to this tool and the transition was remarkably smooth. The onboarding tutorials are comprehensive, and the import from our previous tool worked without a hitch. Six months in, everyone from the engineering team to the sales team is happily using it daily. The workspace templates let each department set up their space exactly how they wanted.

The custom automations are incredibly powerful. I've built workflows that automatically assign reviewers to tasks based on the project area, send weekly digest emails to stakeholders, and archive completed sprints. The automation builder interface is visual and easy to use, even for non-technical team members. My only complaint is that there's a limit on the number of automation runs per month on our plan.

I'm so frustrated with the constant UI changes. Every few weeks, they "improve" the interface and move things around. My team has to relearn where features are located. The most recent update completely changed how subtasks work, breaking our established workflows without any warning or migration path. Product teams: please stop fixing what isn't broken.

Best project management tool I've used in 15 years of managing software teams. The flexibility is unmatched - we use it for everything from bug tracking to OKR management to client project delivery. The database feature essentially gives you a spreadsheet on steroids that integrates with all your project data. The formula capabilities are impressive, and the rollup fields save us hours of manual reporting.

Adequate for basic project management but struggles with complex use cases. We tried using it for our product roadmap and resource allocation, but the lack of proper resource management features forced us to use a separate tool. The dependency management is also too simplistic - real projects have complex dependency chains that this tool can't properly represent.

Solid platform with great potential. The recent addition of the goal-tracking feature was exactly what we needed. Being able to link tasks to strategic objectives gives our team clarity on why their work matters. The dashboards are customizable and look professional enough to present to leadership. I'd love to see better Jira migration support though - the import was rough.

The pricing changes really soured me on this product. We were early adopters on the legacy plan, and they forced us to migrate to a new pricing tier that costs 60% more for essentially the same features. When I complained, they offered a 10% discount as a "loyalty reward." After three years of being a customer and advocate, this is how they treat us. Looking at alternatives now.

The integration ecosystem is extensive. We connect it with Slack, GitHub, Figma, Google Drive, and Zoom, and everything works harmoniously. The two-way sync with Google Calendar means I never miss a deadline. The Zapier integration opens up even more possibilities. Setting up integrations is straightforward and the connection management dashboard makes it easy to troubleshoot when needed.

Our nonprofit has been using this tool for volunteer coordination and event planning. The free tier for nonprofits is generous and much appreciated. The calendar view is perfect for scheduling events, and the form feature lets us collect volunteer signups directly into our project boards. The only issue is that the reporting features on the free tier are quite limited.

I've been using this for personal project management and it's decent for that purpose. The quick-add feature is convenient, and the mobile widget lets me capture ideas on the go. However, I feel like the tool is designed primarily for teams, and the solo user experience feels a bit over-engineered. I don't need workspace management or team features - I just want a powerful personal task manager.

Their API rate limiting is absurd. We built a dashboard that pulls data from multiple projects and hit the rate limit within minutes. When we contacted support about increasing our limits, they said we need to upgrade to the enterprise plan at 3x the cost. The API documentation also has several inaccuracies that cost us development time. Very frustrating experience for a developer-focused use case.

Phenomenal tool for managing our construction projects. We've adapted it for tracking permits, inspections, subcontractor schedules, and material deliveries. The flexibility of the platform means we could customize it for our industry-specific needs without writing any code. The file attachment and image annotation features are particularly useful for documenting site conditions.

Reliable and feature-rich. We've been using it for two years across three departments. The permissions system is granular enough to control access at the task level, which is important for our compliance requirements. The audit trail is useful for tracking changes. I'd like to see better reporting on user activity and adoption metrics for our admin team.

The templates are severely lacking compared to competitors. Most of them are generic and poorly maintained. When I try to use the community templates, half of them are broken or outdated. For a tool that markets itself on "getting started quickly with templates," this is a poor showing. I ended up spending more time fixing a template than I would have building from scratch.

The keyboard shortcuts alone make this worth it. I can fly through my task management without touching the mouse. Cmd+K for quick actions, Cmd+E for recent items, Tab+Q for my tasks - it's like vim for project management. Combined with the powerful markdown support in descriptions and comments, this is a productivity powerhouse for anyone who values efficiency.

Great for managing our editorial calendar. The table view works perfectly for planning content across multiple channels. The custom properties let us track everything from target audience to SEO keywords to publication status. The timeline view helps visualize the publication schedule. Only wish the text formatting options in task descriptions were richer - proper tables and callouts would be nice.

Decent product with some frustrating quirks. The task duplication feature doesn't copy subtasks properly. The recurring tasks feature has a bug where completed recurrences sometimes reappear. The "undo" function only works for the last action and there's no action history. These small issues add up and make the daily experience more frustrating than it needs to be.

We use this to coordinate our global team across 4 time zones and it's been invaluable. The "follow the sun" workflow we've built using automations means work continuously progresses through our Sydney, Mumbai, Stockholm, and New York offices. The time zone display on user profiles and the intelligent notification scheduling ensures nobody gets pinged at 3 AM.

Paid for annual subscription, company went through budget cuts and I needed to cancel. Their cancellation policy is predatory - no refunds, not even prorated. The cancellation process itself was deliberately confusing, requiring multiple steps and a phone call. This kind of dark pattern is unacceptable. Check the cancellation terms very carefully before committing to an annual plan.

The form builder is an underrated feature. We use it to collect bug reports from customers, and they flow directly into our triage board. The conditional logic in forms means we can route issues to the right team automatically. Combined with the automation features, our bug triage process is almost entirely automated. Just wish the forms had more styling options.

It's a capable tool that's held back by inconsistent performance. Some days it's snappy and responsive, other days basic operations take 5-10 seconds to complete. The Chrome extension is particularly unreliable - it frequently fails to sync. I've come to accept these quirks but it's hard to recommend when I can't guarantee the experience will be consistent.

This tool replaced four separate apps for our team: project management, document collaboration, knowledge base, and meeting notes. Having everything in one place has eliminated the context-switching tax we were paying. The linked databases feature means our project data, team wiki, and meeting notes all reference each other seamlessly. It's genuinely transformed our workflow.

Excellent for academic research project management. I use it to track literature reviews, experiment schedules, and paper drafts. The database views let me organize papers by topic, methodology, or relevance. The web clipper browser extension is great for saving research papers directly to my workspace. Would be perfect if it had built-in citation management.

The learning curve is real and the company doesn't do enough to address it. Their "university" is a collection of outdated videos that don't cover recent features. The help docs are organized in a confusing taxonomy. I've spent more time figuring out how to use this tool than actually using it. For what it costs, they should provide better onboarding resources.

Rock-solid reliability. In 18 months of daily use with a team of 25, we've experienced zero downtime and zero data loss. The auto-save is instantaneous, version history is comprehensive, and the backup system gives me peace of mind. In an industry where I've seen competitors lose customer data, this level of reliability is worth every penny.

Average experience. The product does what it promises but lacks the "wow" factor. The UI is clean but feels sterile. The features are functional but uninspired. The integrations work but are basic. It's like the vanilla ice cream of project management - perfectly fine, but you know there are more exciting options out there. If you value stability over innovation, it might be right for you.

This tool has significantly improved our sprint velocity. The sprint planning view with story point estimation and automatic workload distribution is excellent. The burndown chart updates in real-time, and the retrospective template at the end of each sprint keeps our continuous improvement process structured. My only wish is for better integration with testing tools like TestRail.

I cannot believe they still don't have proper offline support in 2026. I work from remote locations frequently and need to access and update my projects without internet. Every competitor offers this. When I raised this on their community forum, the response was "it's on our roadmap." It's been on their roadmap for two years. Actions speak louder than words.

The custom dashboard builder is exceptional. I've created executive dashboards that pull data from across all our projects, showing portfolio health, resource allocation, and budget tracking in one beautiful view. The chart widgets are customizable and the data refreshes in real-time. Our C-suite actually looks at these dashboards, which is a first for any tool we've tried.

Very impressed with the guest access feature. We give our clients limited access to see project progress, leave comments, and approve deliverables. They don't need to create an account or learn the whole tool - just a simple portal view of what's relevant to them. This has dramatically reduced our client communication overhead. The only improvement I'd suggest is allowing more customization of the client portal view.

The tool is fine for basic task management but falls short for portfolio management. When you're trying to manage multiple related projects with resource constraints and cross-project dependencies, the experience gets clunky. The portfolio view is too simplistic and the resource management features are essentially non-existent. They need to decide if they're targeting individual teams or enterprise portfolio management.

I'm a product manager and this has become my "second brain." I use it for everything from user story mapping to roadmap planning to stakeholder communication. The linked databases mean my product requirements, user feedback, and engineering tasks all connect together. The roll-up properties automatically calculate feature completion percentages. It's genuinely made me better at my job.

The export functionality is pathetic. You can export to CSV but it flattens all the rich data into unusable garbage. PDF export looks nothing like the actual views. There's no native Excel export. If you ever need to get your data out of this tool or share it with people who don't use it, you're in for a frustrating time. Data portability should be a basic feature.

Good tool with room for growth. The comment and discussion features are well-designed - threaded conversations, @mentions, emoji reactions, and the ability to resolve threads keeps our communication organized. The activity feed gives a nice overview of what's happening. I'd like to see video comments and better rich-text formatting in discussions.

Switched from Monday.com and the difference is remarkable. The interface is so much cleaner and more logical. The flexibility to create custom views means each team member sees exactly what they need. Performance is consistently fast even with our large dataset of 5000+ tasks. The transition was worth every minute of migration effort.

Middle of the road. The core functionality works reliably. The UI is pleasant. The integrations are adequate. But I keep finding small annoyances: the date picker doesn't support keyboard input, bulk editing is limited to 50 items, you can't create a task from an email without a third-party tool. Death by a thousand paper cuts.

Excellent database capabilities. The relational databases with rollups, lookups, and formulas are incredibly powerful. We've essentially built a custom CRM inside the tool that perfectly fits our workflow. The linked views mean our sales pipeline, customer database, and project tracking all share the same underlying data. Just wish the formula language was better documented.

Data import was a nightmare. We tried to import 5000 tasks from our previous tool and the importer choked halfway through. No error message, no partial import indication, just a spinning wheel that eventually timed out. Support told us to "try importing in smaller batches of 100." Importing 5000 tasks 100 at a time is not a reasonable solution. The import/export infrastructure needs serious work.

The weekl status update feature is brilliant. Every Friday, the tool automatically compiles a status update from each team member's completed tasks, in-progress work, and blockers. It formats it into a beautiful report that goes directly to stakeholders. This alone has saved me 2 hours per week that I used to spend manually compiling updates.

Well-designed tool for managing agile workflows. The customizable board columns, WIP limits, and swimlanes give us the flexibility to implement our specific flavor of Kanban. The cumulative flow diagram is helpful for identifying bottlenecks. The cycle time analytics help us make data-driven improvements to our process. Would love native SAFe support for larger organizations.

The tool works well enough for our needs but the customer success team has been disappointing. After signing our annual contract, our CSM became unresponsive. Feature requests seem to go into a black hole. The product roadmap is opaque - we have no idea what's coming next or when. For what we pay, we expect more communication and partnership from the vendor.

I've been recommending this tool to every founder I mentor. For early-stage startups, it's the perfect all-in-one workspace. You can run your product backlog, wiki, meeting notes, OKRs, and even light CRM all in one place. As you grow, the tool grows with you. The pricing is reasonable for small teams and the feature set punches well above its weight class.

Performance degrades significantly with large databases. Our main project database has about 3000 entries and the table view takes 8-12 seconds to load. Filtering and sorting add additional delays. The calendar view with the same dataset is essentially unusable. We've had to split our data into multiple smaller databases, which defeats the purpose of having a centralized system.

Great Slack integration. The bi-directional sync means we can create tasks from Slack messages and receive task updates in Slack channels. The preview unfurling shows task details right in the chat. The slash commands are intuitive. This tight integration has significantly reduced the friction of switching between communication and task management.

The version history feature has saved us multiple times. Being able to see exactly who changed what and when, and to restore any previous version with a single click, gives us confidence to experiment with our project structures. We once accidentally deleted an entire section of our wiki and had it restored in under a minute. Peace of mind is priceless.

The tool is caught between two identities - it wants to be both a simple task manager and a complex project management suite. The result is a product that's too complicated for simple use cases and too limited for complex ones. I wish they'd pick a lane and excel at it rather than trying to be everything to everyone.

Great for managing our design workflow. We embed Figma files directly into tasks, use the visual approval process for design reviews, and the timeline view helps us plan our design sprints. The ability to create design system documentation alongside our project management is unique and valuable. Would love deeper Figma integration with automatic status updates.

The customer support team deserves a special shoutout. I've had three interactions with them over the past year - a billing question, a feature inquiry, and a bug report. Each time, I received a thoughtful, personalized response within hours. The bug I reported was fixed in the next release and they even followed up to confirm it was resolved. This level of care is rare.

They quietly removed features from our plan without notification. The advanced automation triggers we relied on were moved to a higher tier in their last pricing update. We discovered this when our workflows suddenly stopped working. No email, no in-app notification, no migration period. Just broken workflows and a prompt to upgrade. This is not how you treat paying customers.

The workspace hierarchy (workspace > project > page > database) makes logical sense and helps keep things organized. The breadcrumb navigation is helpful for understanding where you are. The favorites and recent items make it quick to jump to frequently accessed pages. I'd suggest adding a "pinned items" feature for things that need to stay top of mind.

Satisfactory but not exceptional. The product delivers on its basic promise of project and task management. The collaboration features work as advertised. The integrations connect properly. But there's a lack of innovation in recent updates - mostly minor UI tweaks and bug fixes. I'm waiting for something genuinely new and exciting before I can rate higher.

We run our entire company on this platform. Engineering uses it for sprint management, marketing for campaign coordination, sales for pipeline tracking, and HR for recruiting workflows. The fact that one tool can serve all these needs while maintaining data connections between departments is incredibly powerful. It's the closest thing to a custom enterprise application without the development cost.

The recurring tasks feature works really well for our operational processes. We have weekly, monthly, and quarterly recurring tasks that automatically regenerate with all subtasks and assignees intact. The scheduling options are flexible enough to handle complex recurrence patterns. Just wish there was better support for "X business days before deadline" type scheduling.

The search is my biggest pain point. Full-text search across the workspace returns too many irrelevant results. There's no way to search within a specific project or database without navigating there first. The search filters are basic and the results ranking seems random. For a tool that becomes your team's knowledge repository, search should be world-class, not an afterthought.

As a UX researcher, I use this tool to manage research repositories, participant databases, and insight libraries. The linked database feature lets me connect research findings to product features and customer segments. The tagging system helps categorize insights by theme. I've essentially built a research ops platform within the tool at a fraction of the cost of dedicated research tools.

The product is stable and reliable, which I appreciate. No major outages in the year I've been using it. But stability alone doesn't justify the price. The feature development pace feels slow compared to competitors. Each release seems to bring small incremental improvements rather than meaningful new capabilities. I stay because switching costs are high, not because I'm delighted.

The guest collaboration features are well-designed for consulting engagements. We create dedicated client spaces with limited access, share selected pages and databases, and use the commenting system for feedback loops. The guest user doesn't count against our seat limit, which is fair and appreciated. Just need better control over what guests can see in the sidebar.

The API webhooks are a dream to work with. We've set up a comprehensive event-driven architecture where task status changes trigger our CI/CD pipeline, new client entries sync to our CRM, and completed milestones send automated invoices through our billing system. The webhook reliability has been excellent - we haven't missed a single event in six months of production use.

I'm done with this tool. The mobile app just ate three hours of work I entered on the plane because it failed to sync when I got Wi-Fi. No local save, no draft recovery, no conflict resolution. Just gone. This is 2026 - every note-taking app has solved this problem. There's no excuse for a premium productivity tool to lose user data in offline scenarios.

Helpful for managing our community organization's volunteer projects. We coordinate dozens of community events and the tool keeps everything organized. The calendar view synced with Google Calendar ensures volunteers know when and where to show up. The form feature collects volunteer preferences and availability. Great value even on the free tier for non-commercial use.

It's a decent tool that I use daily, but I can't say I enjoy using it. The interface, while clean, feels impersonal and cold. There's no joy in the interaction - no delightful animations, no satisfying task completion moments, no personality. Compare this to apps like Notion or Linear that feel crafted with love, and this tool feels like enterprise software designed by committee.

The table calculations and formula system is surprisingly powerful. We've built financial models, resource allocation algorithms, and automated reporting systems entirely within the tool's database feature. The if/then formulas, date calculations, and cross-database lookups cover 90% of what we previously needed Excel for. It's like having a database engineer on the team.

Good experience overall with a few minor complaints. The drag-and-drop in the board view occasionally glitches when you have more than 6 columns. The text editor sometimes loses formatting when pasting from Google Docs. The @mention autocomplete is slow with large teams. None of these are dealbreakers, but they add friction to the daily experience.

The permissions model is too rigid. We need the ability to share specific tasks with external stakeholders without giving them access to the entire project. The current options are essentially "full access" or "no access" at the project level. Guest access helps but doesn't solve the granular permission needs we have in a regulated industry.

This tool has made me a better manager. The workload view shows me exactly how much each team member has on their plate, the sprint velocity charts help me plan realistic timelines, and the dependency graph prevents me from creating impossible schedules. Data-driven management instead of gut-feel management. Our team morale has improved because we've stopped overcommitting.

The experience varies wildly depending on which feature you're using. The board view is excellent - fast and intuitive. The timeline view is good but has some UX quirks. The form builder is adequate. The document editor is basic. The dashboard is powerful but confusing. It feels like different teams built different features with different design philosophies.

Excellent for managing product launches. We create launch playbooks as templates with all the cross-functional tasks pre-populated. Marketing, engineering, sales, and support all see their responsibilities in one place. The dependent tasks ensure nothing gets missed. The countdown timeline creates urgency and visibility. Our last three launches have been our smoothest ever.

Their "AI assistant" is a gimmick. It generates generic responses that show no understanding of our project context. It suggested "add more team members" when I asked how to speed up a delayed project. The AI summaries are often inaccurate and miss key details. Don't pay extra for the AI features - they're not worth it in their current state.

The workspace templates for different industries are incredibly well-crafted. We used the "Software Development" template as our starting point and it included sprint boards, bug trackers, release notes databases, and architecture decision records - all pre-configured with sensible automations and views. Saved us weeks of setup time.

Solid tool for managing research projects. The ability to create knowledge graphs by linking related pages and databases mirrors how academic research is actually structured. The citation-like linking between documents helps maintain intellectual traceability. The PDF annotation feature is useful for literature reviews. Would benefit from LaTeX support in the text editor.

I have mixed feelings about the recent redesign. The new color scheme is more modern and the simplified navigation is welcome. But they moved several frequently-used functions into sub-menus, adding clicks to common operations. The sidebar collapse behavior is inconsistent between different views. Change for change's sake isn't improvement.

What sets this tool apart is the flexibility. Unlike rigid PM tools that force you into their workflow, this one adapts to how you actually work. Whether you prefer Kanban, Scrum, waterfall, or some hybrid approach, you can set it up exactly the way you want. No compromises, no workarounds. This flexibility is why we've stuck with it for three years.

The onboarding experience has improved significantly since I first tried it a year ago. The interactive tutorials now walk you through creating your first project step by step. The contextual tips that appear when you hover over new features are helpful without being annoying. The "Getting Started" template is a great touch. Still think the advanced features need better documentation.

We experienced a serious security concern when we discovered that deleted guests could still access shared documents through cached links for up to 24 hours. When we reported this to the security team, they downplayed it as "expected behavior due to CDN caching." For a tool handling sensitive project data, this is concerning. They eventually fixed it, but the initial response was troubling.

This tool has become the backbone of our agency's operations. We manage 40+ client accounts with different workflows, templates, and access controls. The ability to templatize our entire client onboarding process and spin up a new workspace in minutes has dramatically reduced our setup time. The white-label client portal is the cherry on top.

The Gantt chart implementation is one of the best I've seen in a web-based tool. The dependency lines are clear, the critical path highlighting is useful, and the resource loading visualization helps identify bottlenecks before they happen. Drag-and-drop rescheduling automatically adjusts dependent tasks. Only missing feature is baseline comparison for tracking schedule variance.

The product has matured considerably since I started using it two years ago. Many of my initial complaints have been addressed. However, new issues have emerged as they've added features. The tool is becoming bloated - there are now three different ways to create a task and two different commenting systems (inline and discussion). Simplification would be welcome.

Complete disaster for enterprise deployment. No SCIM provisioning, limited SAML support, no data residency options, no custom security policies, and their compliance certifications are limited to SOC 2 Type I (not even Type II). Their enterprise tier is priced at enterprise levels but the enterprise features simply aren't there. We wasted three months in an evaluation that should have been eliminated in week one.

I use this tool as a personal productivity system and it's perfect for that. My daily journal, habit tracker, reading list, goal tracking, and project plans all live in one connected workspace. The weekly review template I've built helps me reflect on progress and plan ahead. The mobile widget lets me quickly capture thoughts throughout the day.

The commenting system is well-designed. Inline comments on specific text, threaded discussions on tasks, and the ability to resolve threads keeps conversations organized. The @mention notification system reliably delivers alerts. I especially appreciate the "priority" mention that sends a push notification even when the recipient has notifications silenced. Smart design decision.

This tool and I have a love-hate relationship. I love the concept, the design philosophy, and the flexibility. I hate the performance inconsistencies, the mobile app quality, and the way pricing seems designed to extract maximum value. If they focused on execution quality rather than feature quantity, this could be the best product in the category.

The database relation features are what make this tool special. Creating relational structures between projects, tasks, people, and resources gives us a live, queryable model of our entire organization. Roll-up properties automatically aggregate data up the hierarchy. Filter views slice the data for different audiences. It's essentially a no-code application platform.

The text editor is embarrassingly basic for a modern productivity tool. No proper table support, no callout blocks, limited code block syntax highlighting, no diagram support, and the markdown implementation is incomplete. If your work involves any kind of technical writing, you'll find the editor frustrating. I end up drafting in another tool and pasting in, which is a terrible workflow.

Really appreciate the thought put into keyboard accessibility. Tab navigation works correctly, focus indicators are visible, and screen reader support is above average. As someone who relies on assistive technology, this is one of the more accessible project management tools I've used. There are still some custom components that could use better ARIA labels, but the foundation is solid.

We've reduced our meeting time by 40% since adopting this tool. Status updates happen asynchronously through the weekly check-in feature. Design reviews happen through the commenting system with embedded Figma frames. Sprint planning is faster because the backlog is already prioritized and estimated. The tool hasn't just improved our project management - it's improved our entire way of working.

Functional but uninspiring. The tool does its job and rarely gets in the way. The stability is good, the performance is acceptable, and the feature set covers most needs. But there's nothing that makes me excited to open it in the morning. No "aha" moments, no delightful surprises, just solid utility. Sometimes that's enough, but for this price point, I expect more.

The workflow automation builder has genuinely useful triggers and actions. We've automated new employee onboarding (create task list + assign buddy + schedule meetings), sprint completion (archive done items + generate report + notify stakeholders), and client invoicing (completed milestones trigger invoice generation). The visual builder makes these easy to create and maintain.

I paid for "AI-powered project insights" and received a glorified word cloud. The "risk detection" feature flagged every task with "urgent" in the name, regardless of context. The "smart scheduling" suggestion was to "spread tasks evenly across the week" - thanks, revolutionary. The AI features feel like they were built to check a marketing checkbox, not to solve real problems.

Three years as a customer and I'm still discovering useful features. Just last week, I found the "Dependencies" view that shows a PERT chart of all task relationships in a project. The week before, I learned about the "Timeline Race" feature that compares estimated vs. actual timelines. This depth of functionality, combined with an approachable interface, is rare.

Good value for money. We evaluated five tools side by side and this one offered the best feature-to-price ratio. The mid-tier plan includes features that competitors lock behind enterprise pricing. The free tier is generous enough to properly evaluate the product. No aggressive upselling in the interface. Transparent pricing page. Refreshing in an industry full of "contact sales" buttons.

The product is fine, but the community and ecosystem around it are what really add value. The template gallery has thousands of user-contributed templates. The YouTube tutorial community is active and helpful. The user forum is responsive. The third-party integration marketplace extends functionality significantly. Sometimes the community builds what the product team hasn't gotten to yet.

Hands down the best decision we made this year was switching to this tool. Our project completion rate went from 60% to 88% in the first quarter. Time to market for new features decreased by 30%. Team satisfaction scores on our internal survey went up. The data speaks for itself. Return on investment was clear within the first month.

The tagging system is flexible and powerful. We use a hierarchical tag structure to categorize work by department, initiative, quarter, and priority. The filtered views based on tag combinations give us instant access to exactly the subset of work we need to see. Cross-referencing tags across projects reveals interesting patterns in how work flows through our organization.

The real-time collaboration features are unreliable. At least once a week, someone on our team encounters a sync conflict where their changes are overwritten by another team member's edits. The conflict resolution UI is confusing and sometimes results in data loss. For a tool that emphasizes collaboration, this is a fundamental flaw that needs to be addressed.

The product's approach to data ownership is commendable. Full data export in standard formats, API access to all your data, and clear data retention policies. They don't hold your data hostage to prevent churn. This ethical approach to data handling was actually a deciding factor in our procurement evaluation. More SaaS companies should follow this example.

The tool handles small to medium projects well but struggles with enterprise-scale complexity. Our engineering team of 200 needed advanced features like cross-project resource allocation, capacity planning, and portfolio risk management. These features either don't exist or are too simplistic to be useful. We ended up keeping this tool for individual teams but added a portfolio management layer on top.

The template creation and sharing system is well-designed. We've built a library of internal templates for different project types - product launches, marketing campaigns, engineering sprints, customer onboarding. New team members can spin up properly structured projects in minutes. The template variables feature lets us customize templates at creation time without modifying the master.

Exceptional product. I've been managing projects for 20 years and this is the most complete tool I've used. It respects that every team is different and provides the flexibility to accommodate various methodologies. The recent addition of goal hierarchies (company objectives → team goals → project milestones → tasks) creates perfect alignment from strategy to execution.

The notification system is broken. I receive notifications for projects I left months ago. The "unfollow" button doesn't consistently work. Email notifications often arrive hours after the event. Push notifications on mobile are duplicated. The notification preferences panel has so many options that it's overwhelming, yet somehow none of the combinations produce the behavior I want.

Really solid API documentation. Every endpoint is well-documented with request/response examples, error codes, and rate limit information. The SDKs for Python and JavaScript are maintained and versioned. The API changelog is transparent about breaking changes with adequate deprecation notices. The developer experience is clearly a priority for this team.

We've been using this for our remote-first company of 30 people and it has become our virtual office. Status pages show who's working on what right now. The daily standup bot collects async updates. The team directory with time zones helps us schedule across continents. It's not just project management - it's become our operating system for remote work.

Three stars because it's exactly average. Not the best in any category, not the worst in any category. The UI is nice but not the nicest. The features are comprehensive but not the most comprehensive. The price is reasonable but not the most reasonable. It's the Honda Civic of project management tools - reliable, practical, and utterly unremarkable.

The milestone tracking feature is particularly well-implemented. Milestones sit above tasks in the hierarchy and provide natural checkpoints for project progress. The milestone dashboard shows a timeline of upcoming milestones across all projects. Automated reminders fire when milestones are approaching. The completion celebration animation is a nice touch that the team enjoys.

The GDPR compliance features are inadequate for European businesses. The data processing agreement is buried in their legal page and doesn't cover all the requirements. The data deletion request process is manual and takes up to 30 days. There's no built-in consent management for guest users. We had to implement additional processes around the tool to maintain compliance. Unacceptable for a modern SaaS product.

I taught a university course on project management and used this tool as the practical platform. Students loved it. The free tier for education was perfect. The interface was intuitive enough that students were productive within the first lab session. Several students continued using it for their personal projects after the course ended. That's the ultimate endorsement.

Good tool with excellent uptime. In the 14 months we've been using it, I can recall only one incident where the service was degraded, and it lasted less than an hour. The status page is honest and detailed when issues occur. They proactively communicate via email when planned maintenance is scheduled. This operational transparency builds trust.

The free tier is good enough for personal use but the jump to the paid tier is steep. There's no intermediate option for power users who don't need team features. I'd happily pay $5-8/month for additional personal features like unlimited file uploads and advanced views, but the cheapest paid plan is $15/month and focused on team collaboration I don't need.

The audit log feature on the business plan is exactly what we needed for compliance. Every action is logged with timestamp, user, and details. We can filter by user, action type, or date range. The export functionality lets us send audit data to our compliance team in their required format. This feature alone justified the upgrade to the business plan.

Very capable tool for event management. We coordinate a 3-day conference with 200+ speakers and 50 sessions. The database views help us manage speaker submissions, schedule sessions, track venue logistics, and coordinate volunteer assignments. The timeline view gives us a visual schedule that we can share with attendees. Integration with Eventbrite handles registration.

Support response times have deteriorated dramatically in the past six months. What used to be same-day responses now takes 3-5 business days. When responses come, they're often canned replies that don't address the specific issue. I suspect they've cut their support team as a cost-saving measure. Premium support used to be a selling point; now it's a liability.

The cross-platform experience is seamless. I start planning on my desktop, review updates on my iPad during lunch, and make quick edits from my phone in the evening. The sync is instantaneous and the UI adapts beautifully to each screen size. Each platform feels native rather than like a cramped version of the desktop app. This kind of experience requires real engineering investment.

It does what I need but nothing surprises me anymore. I remember being excited when I first started using it - every week I'd discover a new feature or workflow optimization. Now it feels like the innovation has plateaued. The recent updates have been bug fixes and minor UI tweaks. I understand product maturity, but the competition isn't standing still.

The timeline view with resource allocation is a game-changer for project planning. Being able to see who's overloaded and who has capacity, then drag tasks between team members to balance workload, is incredibly intuitive. The automatic conflict detection warns when someone is double-booked. This visual approach to resource management has reduced our scheduling conflicts by 80%.

Every sprint retrospective, our team agrees: this is the best tool we've ever used. The sprint metrics (velocity, cycle time, lead time) help us continuously improve. The built-in retrospective template captures what went well, what didn't, and action items. The trend charts show our improvement over time. It's made our Scrum practice genuinely effective.

I lost confidence in this product when they had a billing error that charged our company card three times in one month. Getting the refund processed took six weeks and multiple escalations. Their billing system doesn't support purchase orders, which is required by our procurement process. The financial operations of this company don't inspire confidence in the reliability of their product.

The conditional formatting in database views is useful for creating visual priority indicators. Red for overdue, yellow for at risk, green for on track - it makes scanning a large backlog much faster. The custom icons for different task types add another layer of visual organization. These small design touches show attention to detail that I appreciate.

This tool respects my attention. The notification batching, focus mode, and intelligent digests mean I can stay in deep work without being constantly interrupted. The "Do Not Disturb" schedule syncs with my calendar. The notification summary at the end of my focus period catches me up efficiently. In an age of constant distraction, this thoughtful approach to notifications is a competitive advantage.

The tool has become a necessary evil for our team. We don't love it but switching costs are too high. Our workflows, templates, automations, and historical data are all locked in. Even though competitors have caught up or surpassed this tool in several areas, migration would cost us months of productivity. This is the SaaS trap and I resent being in it.

The project health indicators are really helpful. The automatic health score based on overdue tasks, blocked items, and velocity trends gives me an instant read on how each project is doing. The portfolio view aggregates these health scores across all active projects. I can spot troubled projects early and intervene before they become crises. Data-driven project management at its finest.

Been using this for wedding planning and it's been a lifesaver! Tracking vendors, managing the guest list, coordinating with the wedding party, keeping the budget in check - everything in one place. The shared workspace means my partner and I are always on the same page. The countdown timer to the big day is a nice motivational touch. Will definitely keep using it after the wedding for other life projects.

The import from Asana was advertised as "seamless" but it was anything but. Custom fields didn't map correctly, attachments were lost, and the project hierarchy was flattened into a single level. We spent two weeks manually reconstructing our project structure after the import. Their support response was "Asana's data format has limitations" - classic blame deflection.

I manage a book publishing pipeline with this tool and it works beautifully. Each book gets a project board that tracks it from manuscript submission through editing, design, production, and distribution. The template includes all our standard milestones and quality gates. The timeline view shows our entire publishing calendar. Custom fields track ISBN, print run, and distribution channels.

The whiteboard feature they added recently is excellent. We use it for brainstorming sessions, mind mapping, and visual project planning. The ability to convert whiteboard elements directly into tasks in your project board is brilliant - no more lost brainstorming outcomes. The infinite canvas, sticky notes, and drawing tools are smooth and responsive.

This tool is like a relationship - great in the beginning, comfortable in the middle, and slightly disappointing when you realize it's not going to change. The features that excited me initially are now table stakes. The bugs I reported six months ago are still there. The feature requests I submitted have gone unanswered. Loyalty isn't rewarded here.

The reporting capabilities have improved significantly with the latest update. Custom report builders, scheduled report delivery, and the ability to combine data from multiple projects into unified dashboards make it much easier to keep stakeholders informed. The visual design of the reports is professional enough to share directly with executive leadership.

I'm a scrum master and this tool makes my job easier every day. The sprint planning poker feature, burndown charts, velocity tracking, and retrospective boards are all built-in and work seamlessly together. The standup bot that collects async updates and posts a summary saves 15 minutes per day. Our team's agile maturity has improved noticeably since adopting this tool.

The Chrome extension crashed my browser repeatedly. After installing it, Chrome started freezing whenever I had more than 10 tabs open. Uninstalling the extension immediately resolved the issue. I reported the bug and received a generic "we'll look into it" response. Three months later, the same version is still in the Chrome Web Store with no update. Quality control is lacking.

The document hub within each project is well-organized. Meeting notes, specifications, decisions, and references all have their place. The bi-directional linking between documents and tasks creates a web of context that makes it easy to understand why decisions were made. The doc templates for different document types ensure consistency across the team.

This tool handles our complex matrix organization structure better than anything else we've tried. Team members belong to functional departments but work on cross-functional project teams. The multiple workspace membership and cross-workspace search means everyone can find and access what they need regardless of organizational boundaries.

The tool works fine for us but I constantly hear from my team that they prefer the competitor's UI. The design language here is clean but clinical. There's a lack of personality - no custom themes, limited emoji reactions, no fun achievement badges for completing milestones. These seem like small things but they affect daily satisfaction with a tool you use 8 hours a day.

Great for managing construction punch lists and warranty tracking. We've adapted the tool for post-construction workflows, tracking deficiencies by trade, location, and priority. The mobile app (despite its issues with other features) works well enough for field inspectors to log issues with photos. The reporting helps us track resolution rates and hold subcontractors accountable.

What I appreciate most is that this tool doesn't try to replace human judgment with AI automation. The AI features enhance human decision-making rather than trying to make decisions for you. Suggestions are clearly labeled as suggestions. Automations do what you configure them to do - nothing more, nothing less. In an era of overpromising AI, this restraint is refreshing.

The workspace transfer feature is broken and nobody seems to care. We needed to transfer a workspace to a new owner when our PM left the company. The transfer failed silently - no error, no confirmation, just nothing happened. Support's solution was to manually recreate the workspace under the new owner. For a collaboration tool, the admin features are shockingly primitive.

The comment resolution workflow is well-thought-out. Comments can be resolved (hidden but not deleted), reopened if the resolution is disputed, and filtered to show only unresolved items. Each resolved comment keeps a thread of the discussion that led to resolution. This creates an excellent decision log that we reference frequently during project retrospectives.

We use this as our internal knowledge management system and it's transformed how we share institutional knowledge. The wiki-style pages with nested hierarchy, full-text search, and internal linking create a living documentation system. The page analytics show which documentation is most accessed and which pages might be outdated. Our onboarding time for new hires has decreased by 50%.

The automations work when they work, but debugging them when they don't is a nightmare. There's no execution log, no error reporting, and no way to test an automation without actually triggering it in production. I've had automations silently fail for days before I noticed. A simple execution history with success/failure indicators would make a huge difference.

This tool strikes a good balance between power and usability. It's sophisticated enough to handle complex project portfolios but simple enough that non-technical team members can use it without training. The customizable views mean power users can go deep while casual users see a simple task list. This flexibility is what keeps our diverse team all using the same tool.

The dependency management system is surprisingly robust. Beyond simple finish-to-start dependencies, we can set up start-to-start, finish-to-finish, and start-to-finish relationships with lead and lag times. The dependency chain visualization shows the critical path through our project. When a task slips, the cascading impact on dependent tasks is immediately visible.

The pricing page is deliberately confusing. Feature comparisons between tiers are vague, the "per member" vs "per user" distinction is unclear, and the listed price doesn't include the "platform fee" that's revealed at checkout. I felt deceived when our first invoice was 30% higher than what I calculated from the pricing page. Transparency in pricing should be non-negotiable.

The custom fields system is extensive. We use dropdown fields for status, number fields for estimated hours, date fields for deadlines, people fields for assignments, and formula fields for calculated metrics. The ability to group, sort, and filter by any combination of custom fields gives us powerful data organization. Just wish we could have conditional custom fields.

After trying literally every project management tool on the market over my 12-year career, this is the one I've settled on. It's not perfect - nothing is. But it's the most well-rounded, most flexible, and most reliable option available. The team behind it clearly cares about the product and its users. I've renewed our subscription for a third year without hesitation.

I appreciate the product vision but the execution is inconsistent. Some features feel polished and complete while others feel like they were shipped at 70% and never revisited. The whiteboard is great but limited. The forms are useful but basic. The API is powerful but has gaps. I'd rather have fewer features that are fully realized than many half-baked ones.

The resource management features, while not enterprise-grade, are sufficient for our mid-size team. The workload view shows each person's allocation, the capacity indicators highlight overloaded team members, and the rebalancing suggestions help me redistribute work. Combined with the time tracking integration, we have good visibility into team utilization.

Their marketing says "enterprise-ready" but the reality is far from it. No custom SLAs, no dedicated instance, no data residency choices, limited SSO options, no custom contract terms. Every enterprise requirement was met with "that's on our roadmap" or "we can discuss that on the enterprise plan." A plan that costs $40/user/month should deliver enterprise features, not promises.

Five stars specifically for the API and developer experience. The REST API is comprehensive, the GraphQL endpoint is performant, the webhook system is reliable, and the SDK is well-maintained. We've built three internal tools on top of the API and the experience has been excellent. Rate limits are generous on our plan and the API team is responsive to feedback.

The tool handles our complex approval workflows nicely. We've set up multi-stage approvals where content moves from draft to review to legal to final approval, with different approvers at each stage. The approval history is tracked, comments are linked to specific versions, and automated notifications keep the process moving. It's replaced our email-based approval chaos.

This review contains some "special characters" to test things: angle brackets <like this>, ampersands & symbols, and even some unicode: cafe, resume, naive. The tool handles most of these fine in task descriptions but search sometimes fails to find text with accented characters. Also the CSV export mangles any text with commas or line breaks in it.

The tool's approach to permissions is well-balanced. Workspace-level roles provide broad access control, while project-level permissions allow fine-tuning. The "Comment only" role is perfect for stakeholders who need to observe and provide feedback without modifying project structure. The permission audit view shows exactly what each person can access, which our security team loves.

The Slack integration deserves its own review. The bi-directional sync is reliable, the slash commands are intuitive, and the message actions (turn a message into a task) work seamlessly. The channel-to-project linking keeps relevant updates flowing to the right channels. The daily digest bot posts a customizable summary of project activity. It's clearly built by people who understand how teams actually communicate.

We hit the automation limit on our plan and were forced to upgrade to a plan that's double the price just to get more automation runs. The automation pricing should be decoupled from the overall plan pricing. We don't need any of the other features on the higher tier - just more automations. This kind of bundling feels like it's designed to maximize revenue rather than serve customer needs.

Great for managing our nonprofit's grant application pipeline. Each grant opportunity gets a card that moves through our evaluation stages. The custom fields track deadlines, funding amounts, eligibility criteria, and alignment with our mission. The calendar view shows all upcoming deadlines. The document attachments keep proposals and supporting materials organized. An affordable, flexible solution for resource-constrained organizations.

I've been a paying customer for 4 years and have watched this product evolve from a simple task manager into a comprehensive work management platform. The development team's pace of innovation is impressive while maintaining backward compatibility. I've never had a feature break due to an update. My original workflows still function perfectly alongside all the new capabilities.

The product is in a mature phase and it shows. Innovation has slowed, the UI hasn't had a meaningful refresh in over a year, and the feature requests forum is full of highly-voted items that remain unaddressed. I understand that stability has value, but the gap between this tool and the competition is narrowing. Some investment in innovation would be reassuring.

Really appreciate the offline mode they finally added to the desktop app. Working on flights and in areas with spotty connection is now possible. Changes sync seamlessly when connectivity is restored. The conflict resolution when multiple offline edits overlap is handled gracefully. This was a long-requested feature and the implementation is solid. Now if only the mobile app had the same capability.

Our design team uses this tool in ways that would surprise the product team. We've built design critique workflows, asset management databases, design token libraries, and component documentation systems. The embedded Figma files, image galleries in databases, and visual kanban boards make it feel like a tool built for designers, even though it's a general-purpose PM tool.

The tool occasionally has moments of brilliance - like when the AI suggested linking two seemingly unrelated tasks that actually had a dependency I'd missed. But these moments are rare and surrounded by average-to-mediocre experiences. The AI features feel experimental rather than production-ready. I'd rather they waited and released something consistently good than what we have now.

Solid tool for managing our supply chain operations. We track shipments, manage vendor relationships, and coordinate warehouse operations all within the platform. The database relations connect purchase orders to suppliers, shipments, and inventory records. The timeline view shows expected delivery dates against production schedules. Custom fields track lot numbers and compliance certificates.

The weekly standup automation has been transformative for our distributed team. Every Monday morning, the tool sends each team member a brief form asking about priorities, blockers, and availability. Responses are compiled into a team standup summary that's posted to our Slack channel by 10 AM. Async standup that takes 2 minutes instead of a 30-minute video call. Brilliant.

They've created a product that's intentionally difficult to leave. The export options are limited, the data format is proprietary, and there's no simple way to migrate to another tool. When I asked about bulk export, support suggested I use the API to extract data one item at a time. This vendor lock-in strategy may retain customers in the short term but builds resentment.

The project templates marketplace is a goldmine. We found templates for everything from product launch playbooks to employee onboarding checklists to quarterly business reviews. The quality varies, but the top-rated templates are genuinely well-structured and save significant setup time. The ability to preview a template before applying it prevents disappointing surprises.

This tool has made our OKR process actually work. Company objectives cascade to team key results which cascade to individual tasks. Progress rolls up automatically from task completion to key result progress to objective health. The quarterly OKR review template facilitates structured discussions. We finally have alignment from strategy to execution.

I appreciate the clean design but sometimes form follows function too aggressively. Important actions are hidden behind minimalist menus. Status indicators are subtle to the point of being invisible. The monochrome icon set makes it hard to distinguish between different action types at a glance. A little more visual variety would improve usability without sacrificing aesthetics.

The calendar integration is well-executed. Two-way sync with Google Calendar means task deadlines show up on my calendar and calendar events can be linked to project tasks. The meeting notes feature automatically creates a document linked to the calendar event and pre-populates the attendee list. The time blocking feature helps me protect focused work time.

After evaluating this tool with our security team, I can confidently say the security posture is strong. SOC 2 Type II certification, encryption at rest and in transit, regular penetration testing, and a well-run bug bounty program. The admin controls for data loss prevention, including attachment restrictions and domain-based sharing rules, meet our corporate security requirements.

I'm writing this review while staring at a loading spinner that's been going for 4 minutes. This happens at least twice a day. The "Performance improvements" they announced last quarter have made no noticeable difference. Our workspace has about 1000 pages and 5000 tasks - this is not an unreasonable amount of data. If the architecture can't handle this scale, they shouldn't sell to teams that will reach it.

The dark mode implementation is excellent. Not just an inverted color scheme but a thoughtfully designed dark theme with appropriate contrast ratios, reduced eye strain, and consistent styling across all views. The scheduled switching between light and dark modes based on time of day is a nice touch. Accessibility features in general are above average for this category.

We use the database relations to track our entire hiring pipeline. Candidates are linked to positions, which are linked to departments, which are linked to budget approvals. Each candidate card tracks interview stages, interviewer feedback, and offer status. Roll-up properties show pipeline health metrics. We built this without writing a single line of code. Remarkable flexibility.

I switched from the competitor specifically for the AI features after their marketing blitz about "AI-powered project management." The reality is underwhelming. The AI can summarize tasks and suggest due dates, but it can't do anything intelligent like identify project risks, predict delays, or suggest resource optimizations. The AI is more of a writing assistant than a project management assistant.

The filtered views are incredibly useful. I have views set up for "My overdue tasks," "Blocked items across all projects," "This week's priorities," and "Unassigned work." These personal views give me instant access to exactly the information I need without navigating through project structures. The shared views ensure my team is looking at the same filtered data during meetings.

The tool's commitment to accessibility is genuine, not performative. Screen reader support is comprehensive, keyboard navigation covers all functionality, color contrast meets WCAG AAA standards, and the responsive design works beautifully with screen magnification. As an accessibility advocate, I'm impressed by the attention to detail. The accessibility team clearly includes people with disabilities in their testing.

Their AI chatbot support is useless. Every time I ask a question, I get a generic answer that doesn't address my specific situation. When I ask to speak to a human, I'm told to "submit a ticket" which takes 3-5 business days to get a response. The human responses often just link to help articles I've already read. The support experience has declined significantly over the past year.

The kanban board is one of the most polished implementations I've used. Cards are visually rich with thumbnails, progress indicators, and priority badges. The swimlane grouping by assignee, priority, or any custom field adds another dimension of organization. The WIP limits with visual warnings help us maintain flow. The board-level automation triggers are powerful and easy to configure.

First PM tool that our whole company actually uses. Previous tools were adopted by PMs and ignored by everyone else. The key difference is the low barrier to entry - the simple task list view doesn't overwhelm casual users while power users can switch to more complex views. The gentle learning curve means adoption happens naturally rather than being forced.

The recent price increase without any corresponding feature improvements has left a bad taste. We've been paying for this tool for two years and the experience hasn't meaningfully improved, but our bill has gone up 25%. The "new features" listed in the price increase justification email were features we already had access to. This kind of communication erodes trust.

The goal tracking feature bridges the gap between strategy and execution nicely. We set quarterly objectives, link them to measurable key results, and connect those to the actual tasks that drive progress. The visual progress indicators roll up from tasks to key results to objectives. Our leadership team finally has visibility into how strategic initiatives are progressing. Would love to see OKR scoring built in.
